ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) ProxyLogon-era webshell execution: w3wp.exe spawning cmd/PowerShell on Exchange
ucs.description: Detects child processes spawned by the IIS / Exchange worker process w3wp.exe — the textbook post-ProxyLogon (CVE-2021-26855/26857/26858/27065) execution primitive used by Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM where a dropped China Chopper-style .aspx webshell (e.g. UMWorker) under \FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ runs OS commands as the IIS app pool identity.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","nltest.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe","hostname.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where match(parent_process,"(?i)(MSExchange|owa|ecp|oab|FrontEnd\\\\HttpProxy)") OR match(dest,"(?i)(exch|cas|mbx|mail)") | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
// HAFNIUM / Silk Typhoon — w3wp.exe (Exchange/IIS worker) spawning OS command interpreters
// Pair with co-occurring .aspx file drops in \FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ for highest fidelity.
let _suspicious_children = dynamic([
    "cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe",
    "whoami.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","nltest.exe",
    "tasklist.exe","quser.exe","hostname.exe","reg.exe",
    "sc.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","wmic.exe",
    "schtasks.exe","makecab.exe","rundll32.exe"
  ]);
let _exec_signals = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
    | where FileName in~ (_suspicious_children)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"            // skip machine-context noise
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
              ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
              ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              ChildImage  = FolderPath,
              ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
              ChildName   = FileName,
              SHA256;
// Optional enrichment — webshell file drops by the same w3wp.exe inside Exchange paths
let _shell_drops = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified")
    | where FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".ashx"
    | where FolderPath has_any (@"\inetpub\wwwroot\",
                                @"\Exchange Server\",
                                @"FrontEnd\HttpProxy\",
                                @"\ClientAccess\",
                                @"\OAB\")
    | project DropTime = Timestamp, DeviceName, DroppedFile = FileName, DroppedPath = FolderPath;
_exec_signals
| join kind=leftouter _shell_drops on DeviceName
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// HAFNIUM / Silk Typhoon — w3wp.exe spawning command interpreters (4688 process-create)
// Requires the GPO 'Include command line in process creation events' enabled.
let _suspicious_children = dynamic([
    "cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe",
    "whoami.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","nltest.exe",
    "tasklist.exe","quser.exe","hostname.exe","reg.exe",
    "sc.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","wmic.exe",
    "schtasks.exe","makecab.exe","rundll32.exe"
  ]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith "w3wp.exe"
| extend ChildBin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,"\\")[-1]))
| where ChildBin in (_suspicious_children)
| where Account !endswith "$"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, ChildBin, CommandLine,
          TokenElevationType, SubjectUserSid
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) Webshell Command Execution via IIS Worker (w3wp.exe)
id: 6f1b3c7a-2a5b-4f6c-8a3a-silktyph0001
status: stable
description: |
  Detects child processes spawned by the IIS/Exchange worker process w3wp.exe — the canonical execution
  primitive used by Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM after dropping China Chopper-style .aspx webshells via
  ProxyLogon (CVE-2021-26855/26857/26858/27065) and related Exchange / IIS exploitation.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/06
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.g0125
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\quser.exe'
      - '\hostname.exe'
      - '\reg.exe'
      - '\sc.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\schtasks.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child and not filter_machine_acct
falsepositives:
  - Exchange CU / patching scripts that legitimately invoke cmd / powershell from w3wp app pool
  - Custom OWA / ECP integrations
level: high
ucs.rationale: w3wp.exe spawning a command interpreter is the single most reliable signal of webshell-driven RCE on an Exchange / IIS host, and is THE behaviour Microsoft, CISA, FireEye/Mandiant and Volexity all flagged as the HAFNIUM signature when Operation Exchange Marauder broke in March 2021. The IIS app pool identity has no legitimate reason to launch cmd.exe, net.exe, nltest.exe, etc. — combined with .asp
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon LSASS credential dump → 7zip staging chain (procdump / comsvcs MiniDump + archive)
ucs.description: Hunts the actor-specific post-exploitation chain Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM ran on compromised Exchange and pivot hosts: Sysinternals procdump (or rundll32 + comsvcs.dll MiniDump) against lsass.exe, followed within hours by a 7zip / RAR archive (often password-protected with -hp / -mhe) on the same host to stage the dump for exfiltration.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t min(_time) as dump_time from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where ((Processes.process_name IN ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe") AND Processes.process="*lsass*") OR (Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" AND Processes.process="*comsvcs.dll*MiniDump*") OR (Processes.process="*lsass*" AND Processes.process="*-ma*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename process as dump_cmd, process_name as dump_bin | join type=inner dest [ | tstats summariesonly=t min(_time) as archive_time from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("7z.exe","7za.exe","rar.exe","winrar.exe") (Processes.process="* a *" OR Processes.process="*-hp*" OR Processes.process="*-p*" OR Processes.process="*-mhe*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename process as archive_cmd, process_name as archive_bin ] | where archive_time >= dump_time AND archive_time <= (dump_time + 14400) | eval delay_min = round((archive_time - dump_time)/60,1) | table dest user dump_time dump_bin dump_cmd archive_time archive_bin archive_cmd delay_min | sort - dump_time
ucs.kql:
// Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM — LSASS dump (procdump or comsvcs.dll MiniDump) followed within 4h
// by a 7zip / RAR archive on the same host (the canonical credential-staging chain).
let _window = 4h;
let _lsass_dump = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where (FileName in~ ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe")
             and ProcessCommandLine has "lsass")
         or (FileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
             and ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs.dll"
             and ProcessCommandLine has "MiniDump")
         or (ProcessCommandLine has "lsass"
             and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-ma ", "-accepteula", "MiniDumpWriteDump"))
    | project DumpTime    = Timestamp,
              DeviceId, DeviceName,
              DumpAccount = AccountName,
              DumpBin     = FileName,
              DumpCmd     = ProcessCommandLine,
              DumpParent  = InitiatingProcessFileName;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName in~ ("7z.exe","7za.exe","rar.exe","winrar.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (" a ", "-hp", "-mhe", " -p", " -p\"")
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| join kind=inner _lsass_dump on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (DumpTime .. DumpTime + _window)
| extend DelayMinutes = datetime_diff('minute', Timestamp, DumpTime)
| project DumpTime, DeviceName, DumpAccount, DumpBin, DumpCmd, DumpParent,
          ArchiveTime = Timestamp,
          ArchiveAccount = AccountName,
          ArchiveBin     = FileName,
          ArchiveCmd     = ProcessCommandLine,
          DelayMinutes
| order by DumpTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Silk Typhoon — LSASS dump + 7zip / RAR staging on the same Windows host within 4h.
let _window = 4h;
let _lsass_dump = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend Bin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,"\\")[-1]))
    | where (Bin in ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe") and CommandLine has "lsass")
         or (Bin == "rundll32.exe" and CommandLine has "comsvcs.dll" and CommandLine has "MiniDump")
         or (CommandLine has "lsass" and CommandLine has_any ("-ma ","MiniDumpWriteDump"))
    | project DumpTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, DumpAccount = Account,
              DumpBin = Bin, DumpCmd = CommandLine,
              DumpParent = ParentProcessName;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ArchiveBin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,"\\")[-1]))
| where ArchiveBin in ("7z.exe","7za.exe","rar.exe","winrar.exe")
| where CommandLine has_any (" a ","-hp","-mhe"," -p")
| join kind=inner _lsass_dump on Computer
| where TimeGenerated between (DumpTime .. DumpTime + _window)
| extend DelayMinutes = datetime_diff('minute', TimeGenerated, DumpTime)
| project DumpTime, Computer, DumpAccount, DumpBin, DumpCmd, DumpParent,
          ArchiveTime = TimeGenerated, ArchiveAccount = Account,
          ArchiveBin, ArchiveCmd = CommandLine, DelayMinutes
| order by DumpTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) LSASS Credential Dump via procdump or comsvcs.dll MiniDump
id: 8b2fe2e3-9d3a-4cda-9b2e-silktyph0002
status: stable
description: |
  Detects the LSASS memory-dump primitive Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM ran post-Exchange-exploitation
  to harvest credentials before lateral movement and exfiltration via 7zip. Covers Sysinternals
  procdump (renamed or not) and the rundll32 + comsvcs.dll MiniDump LOLBin variant.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1003/001/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/06
tags:
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1003.001
  - attack.t1003
  - attack.g0125
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  procdump_lsass:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\procdump.exe'
      - '\procdump64.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: 'lsass'
  comsvcs_minidump:
    Image|endswith: '\rundll32.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'comsvcs.dll'
      - 'MiniDump'
  generic_minidump_lsass:
    CommandLine|contains: 'lsass'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'MiniDumpWriteDump'
      - '-ma '
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: (procdump_lsass or comsvcs_minidump or generic_minidump_lsass) and not filter_machine_acct
falsepositives:
  - Authorised IR / forensic memory captures (whitelist by host + ticket)
  - Microsoft support engineers using procdump for crash diagnostics
level: high
ucs.rationale: Microsoft, Volexity and Mandiant all attribute this exact two-stage tool chain to HAFNIUM / Silk Typhoon: procdump.exe -ma lsass.exe (or the rundll32 comsvcs.dll MiniDump LOLBin equivalent) immediately followed by a 7zip / RAR archive — frequently password-protected with -hp — staged on the compromised Exchange or pivot host. Either step alone is suspicious; the time-windowed correlation on the sa
